CS0234: Missing Aspose.Words.Comments na.error: The type or namespace name 'Comments' does not exist in the namespace 'Aspose.Words' (are you missing an assembly reference?)
CS0234: Missing Aspose.Words.Comments na.fix: The Comment class is defined directly in the Aspose.Words namespace; there is no separate Aspose.Words.Comments namespace. Remove the erroneous using directive and rely on Aspose.Words only.
CS0234: Missing Aspose.Words.Comments na.code:
using Aspose.Words; // correct
// using Aspose.Words.Comments; // removed
CS0234: Missing Aspose.Words.Comments na.note: Aspose.Words 23.x and later expose Comment, CommentRangeStart, etc., directly under Aspose.Words. Adding a non‑existent namespace causes a compile‑time error.

CS8625: Null literal to non‑nullable ref.error: Cannot convert null literal to non‑nullable reference type for the parameter 'author'.
CS8625: Null literal to non‑nullable ref.fix: Mark the author parameter as nullable (string?) and keep the default value null. Adjust the null‑check accordingly.
CS8625: Null literal to non‑nullable ref.code: public static List<string> ExtractComments(string docxPath, string? author = null)
CS8625: Null literal to non‑nullable ref.note: With C# 8+ nullable reference types enabled, a plain string cannot be assigned null. Declaring it as string? tells the compiler the value may be null.
